title: Quadratic Equations and Complex Numbers
subject: Mathematics
content:
# Quadratic Equations and Complex Numbers

## Quick Synopsis

Quadratics and complex numbers are algebra staples. JEE asks root location, parameter ranges, modulus-argument geometry, and transformations in the Argand plane.

## Must Know

- Discriminant controls nature of roots.
- Sum and product of roots connect coefficients to roots.
- Location of roots uses sign of quadratic at points plus vertex position.
- Complex number z = x + iy corresponds to point (x, y).
- Modulus is distance from origin; argument is angle from positive real axis.
- Conjugate helps rationalize and handle modulus.

## Formulas / Rules

- Roots of ax² + bx + c = 0: x = (-b ± sqrt(b² - 4ac))/2a.
- α + β = -b/a; αβ = c/a.
- |z| = sqrt(x² + y²); arg z = tan inverse(y/x) with quadrant.
- z zbar = |z|².
- Polar form: z = r(cosθ + i sinθ).
- De Moivre: [r(cosθ + i sinθ)]^n = r^n(cos nθ + i sin nθ).

## JEE Focus

- For parameter questions, combine discriminant and interval conditions.
- Use completing square to locate quadratic range.
- Use geometry for |z - a| = constant circles and |z - a| = |z - b| perpendicular bisectors.
- Roots of unity form regular polygons on unit circle.

## Common Traps

- Argument is multi-valued; principal argument has specified range.
- Discriminant ≥ 0 gives real roots but not necessarily roots in required interval.
- Modulus inequalities represent regions, not just equations.
- i powers cycle every 4.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you solve root location problems?
- Can you use Vieta relations?
- Can you convert rectangular and polar form?
- Can you interpret complex equations geometrically?
